title: Congress demands withdrawal of Haj circular seeking additional payment from pilgrims
content: Congress Rajya Sabha MP Nasser Hussain seeks full refund of all amounts collected under the circular and structural reforms of Ministry of Minority Affairs’ management of pilgrimage.

title: Vinesh flags fear of bias, says government to be responsible for any incident in Gonda
content: If any untoward incident happens to me, my team or supporters during the competition, the Indian government will be responsible, says Vinesh Phogat

title: Assam polls verdict on May 4; counting to decide BJP hat-trick or Congress comeback
content: EVMs, holding the electoral fates of 722 hopefuls from 126 Assembly constituencies of the State, will be opened at 40 counting centres across all 35 districts amid tight security arrangements

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: Kerala poll turnout rises to 79.7% after inclusion of service voters’ postal ballots, says CEO
content: The Chief Electoral Officer said 53,984 postal ballots had been issued to service voters, of which 20,028 had been received as of May 1.

title: Independent media outlets and online critics: Far from a safe harbour
content: An infrastructure of censorship is emerging, enabling the Central and various State governments to take social media content down using existing laws and rules. Independent media outlets and online critics now find their reach at a precipice. Aroon Deep reports on how in an environment of misinformation and deepfakes, opposing voices also become targets

title: Apple says India antitrust body overstepping judicial authority as spat intensifies
content: The Competition Commission ​of India has since ‌2024 sought Apple’s financial information, typically needed to calculate penalties, after an investigation found it abused its dominant position

title: Above-normal heat likely in Telangana in May; rainfall may offer partial relief: IMD
content: The IMD has indicated that many parts of southern peninsular India, which includes Telangana, are expected to record higher-than-normal daytime temperatures during the month

title: AIADMK urges ECI to prevent unauthorised entry into counting centres
content: AIADMK functionary and Rajya Sabha MP I.S. Inbadurai flagged alleged "misuse of press passes to gain unauthorized entry into counting centres"

title: Silence, the court is in session
content: The resignation of Allahabad High Court judge Yashwant Varma last month, amid parliamentary proceedings to remove him over allegations that burnt wads of unaccounted cash were found at his Delhi residence in 2025, has brought into focus how the judiciary holds itself to account. Aaratrika Bhaumik reports on the opacity surrounding complaints against judges, the routine rejection of information requests, and the shrinking space for criticism

title: Why ‘Moondram Pirai’ still tugs at our heartstrings more than 40 years on
content: One of the greatest-ever Tamil films, ‘Moondram Pirai’ paired Kamal Haasan and Sridevi, both at their histrionic best, and was splendidly directed by Balu Mahendra

title: Only general medical services to be provided at Srisailam temple hospital on Monday
content: The two Apollo doctors currently serving at the Devasthanam Hospital are on leave on Monday (May 4) to appear for FEM examination, regular services to be resumed from Tuesday

title: DMK writes to ECI to ensure peaceful counting of votes
content: The DMK has called on the ECI to take immediate preventive steps, including deploying adequate security personnel and coordinating closely with law enforcement agencies

title: TTD board member demands action on the accused named by one-man panel
content: The committee headed by retired bureaucrat Dinesh Kumar submitted its report to the State government recently after probing the issue in depth to identify the anomalies and loopholes that led to the irregularities

title: Nexus between narcotic dealers, terrorists challenge for country's internal security: J&K L-G
content: Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha said the administration has announced a war against drugs in Jammu and Kashmir, and it will rescue and save the youth from drug abuse

title: Ninth class student plays kidnap drama to skip tuition
content: The boy, a resident of Housing Board Colony, failed in the ninth class examinations. Parents shifted him to other school and arranged tuition during summer vacations

title: Raghav Chadha | The prodigy who walked away
content: The Rajya Sabha MP, who was one of the close confidants of Kejriwal, now says the ‘toxic work culture’ of AAP forced him to quit the party and join the BJP

title: Tamizh Amudhan Sukumar Azhikode centenary award for Medha Patkar and Perumal Murugan
content: The selection committee was headed by K. T. Thomas, and included M. N. Karasseri, Ravi Dece, and Paul Manalil

title: Parliamentary forum discussion needed for Great Nicobar projects, says Congress
content: The Opposition party claimed that the Modi government is "rattled" and in damage control mode after Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi's visit to Great Nicobar last week

title: West Bengal Assembly polls: Ruckus outside counting centre in Mamata’s constituency
content: Trinamool Congress workers alleged two cars bearing the BJP's flag were allowed entry to the compound where EVMs are kept in Bhabanipur

title: Honey trap case: SP warns action against spreading rumours on social media
content: Mr. Jagadeesh warned of initiating legal action against anyone found circulating unverified news or rumours regarding the case on social media

title: MP boat tragedy: Death toll climbs to 13; body of last missing person recovered
content: The boat, around 20 years old and operated by the State tourism department, capsized during a storm on April 30, 2026

title: News in Frames: Ban season, but the catch holds
content: Even as the annual fishing ban remains in force, some fishermen are sustaining livelihoods within permitted limits; using small fish as bait, they are landing high-value catch and earning during the ban

title: How to see election results of Assembly polls 2026? A step-by-step guide
content: The counting of votes for 2026 Assembly elections in Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Assam, and Puducherry begins at 8.00 a.m. on Monday, May 4.

title: Memorial for tusker Arjuna to be inaugurated on May 6
content: Arjuna had won the hearts of many as he carried the golden howdah during the Mysuru Dasara procession eight times.

title: Missing: Chennai’s new master plan
content: The Third Master Plan for Chennai is meant to create a city that would be future-ready and in sync with all the requirements of a modern metropolis. The CMDA was expected to release the plan with the government’s approval during Pongal 2026. However, there is no sign of the plan yet, as the city awaits creative reimagining

title: TNEA 2026 process kicks off with release of tentative schedule
content: The certificate verification process will be held between June 8 and June 20 following which, the rank list will be released on June 29

title: ‘Ten lifetimes won’t be enough’: TMC’s Abhishek Banerjee dares ‘bangla birodhi’ leaders to contest from Falta
content: Come to Falta. Send your strongest, send one of the godfathers from Delhi. If you have got the nerve, contest in Falta, says TMC general secretary Abhishek Banerjee

title: Opposing campaigns in Kerala map out the best- and worst-case scenarios for their respective alliances
content: Exit polls favouring UDF leave the Opposition feeling bullish. LDF feels voters will stay the course as in the past Assembly elections, preferring “stability over volatility”. The BJP-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is hoping to shrink the UDF and LDF’s margins by positioning itself as a Central government-backed development alternative to the traditional fronts in Kerala.

title: Watch: What happens on counting day? | Explained
content:
Voting is over. Across Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Puducherry, West Bengal and Assam, the big question now is: who wins? 
With counting of votes set to begin on May 4, here’s a closer look at how the process is done in India — step by step.

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Election is not a boxing contest, but a fight between ideologies: Riyas
content: Responding to UDF candidate P.V. Anvar’s dare to shave his head if defeated in the election, Minister says even UDF workers will not accept such challenges

title: 83.96% men, 86.2% women voted at polling stations in Assembly poll across Tamil Nadu: EC
content: A total of 4,87,98,833 voters out of 5,73,43,291 turned up to exercise their franchise on April 23 in T.N. Assembly polls; total votes polled may go up as the current figures do not include postal ballots or senior citizens who voted through Form 12D

title: Delhi records minimum temperature of 24.6°C, thunderstorms with rain likely
content: The IMD on Saturday (May 2, 2026) issued a 'yellow' alert for the national capital for the next two days, warning of rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds. The cloudy skies and intermittent rain are expected to persist till May 6

title: EC makes final preparations for counting of votes at 62 centres across Tamil Nadu
content: Chief Electoral Officer Archana Patnaik holds review meeting with with Election Commission officials ahead of counting day on May 4

title: West Bengal election: Hundreds stage protest over ‘threats’ by TMC workers in Falta
content: The CRPF deputy commandant visited the area with a large force and assured the demonstrators of ensuring their safety.

title: High turnout for repoll in 15 West Bengal booths; peaceful amid tight security
content: Protests erupt in Falta over BJP’s allegations of malpractice and voter harassment, with a decision on repolls in the seat still pending

title: India’s first State-led Centre of Excellence for space tech launched in Bengaluru
content: The idea is to drive advanced research and innovation in space technologies, industry-aligned training and talent development, startup incubation and ecosystem building and cross-sector applications of

title: SCR seeks land at four locations in Telangana for mega coaching terminals
content: Key infrastructure bottlenecks identified at a meeting includes long-pending proposal to widen the vent on Tank Bund–Ranigunj road, construction of an additional ROB in Mallapur

title: Elephant parading rules violated rampantly, alleges animal task force
content: Activists blame systemic lapses for deaths in Irinjalakuda and Angamaly; demand strict enforcement of norms

title: Tribals, Scheduled Castes preserved India's identity and soul despite foreign invasions: Bhagwat
content: "This is the society's enduring ethos, which has survived for thousands of years. For various reasons, partly because of our indifference and partly because of foreign invasion, those who preserved this ethos paid a heavy price," says Mohan Bhagwat

title: High speed mechanised boats proposed for Nellore fishermen
content: The absence of such larger boats along the State’s coastline is widely felt as the reason for poaching from the neighbouring States

title: Rahul Gandhi cites Gujarat BJP chief’s ‘indecent remarks’ against Geniben Thakor
content: Mr. Gandhi says the controversy exposes the BJP’s anti-women and Manuwadi mindset and questions the party’s commitment to empowering women

title: Access to generic Risdiplam sought under policy for rare diseases for people with Spinal Muscular Atrophy
content: The recent availability of India-manufactured generic Risdiplam offers a practical pathway, patients write in an appeal to the PM; access to treatment is time sensitive, with delays having the potential to cause irreversible deterioration in the life-limiting neuromuscular condition

title: Jabalpur cruise tragedy: Death toll rises to 11 as bodies of two kids found; search continues
content: Two more missing people from the capsized cruise boat are yet to be found; M.P. Tourism Secretary says a revised SOP will be prepared to prevent such incidents in future

title: Stuck for over an hour, no response from police, says woman who confronted Maharashtra Minister for traffic jam
content: Teena Choudhry, the woman who confronted Maharashtra Minister Girish Mahajan over a traffic jam, has released an account of the incident, saying said she was stuck near Mahindra Taaz for more than an hour and received no response from police officers

title: Social security measures for construction workers soon, says A.P. Minister
content: Proposals have been submitted for a 500-bed secondary hospital and a 150-bed superspecialty ESIC medical college in Amaravati, says Subhash

title: Court denies bail to woman accused in TCS Nashik case
content: The accused is now likely to appeal against the decision in the Bombay High Court. Four others arrested in the case have been remanded to police custody till May 5

title: ECI announces repolling for Falta Assembly seat in West Bengal on May 21
content: In a notification, the ECI says multiple complaints of electoral malpractices were received on the day of polling in the constituency. The votes will be counted on May 24

title: 'I'm one of six victims harassed by Brij Bhushan': Vinesh Phogat drops bombshell
content: "Due to certain circumstances, I want to tell you all something. I did not want to speak while the case is still pending... But I want to say that I myself am one of those six victims who filed a complaint, and our testimonies are still ongoing."

title: Kerala's power 'cycle' put to test: Will Left keep rolling or UDF take the seat?
content: Estimates vary, but the range broadly suggests the UDF could cross the halfway mark in the 140-member House, with projections spanning from around 70 seats at the lower end to close to 90 at the higher end. In contrast, the LDF is largely seen falling short, with projections indicating a ceiling below a simple majority. If these projections hold, the outcome would carry significance beyond a routine change of government.

title: As Accenture rolls out Microsoft Copilot to staff, CEO says teams doing higher-value work
content: Microsoft's Copilot 365 AI assistant is now powering Accenture's workforce of 743,000 employees, marking the largest enterprise deal for the chatbot. This significant adoption by Accenture, a leading AI adopter, highlights Microsoft's strategy to boost paid Copilot subscriptions. Early results show employees completing routine tasks up to 15 times faster, with major productivity gains reported.

title: Watch: Diljit confronts fans waving pro-Khalistan flags, halts Canada concert
content: Diljit Dosanjh paused his Calgary concert amid pro-Khalistan flags, addressing criticism. He defended his efforts to highlight Punjab's issues on platforms like Kaun Banega Crorepati and The Tonight Show, stating his focus remains on his homeland. This follows past controversy with Sikhs For Justice over his interactions with Amitabh Bachchan.

title: Laugh in the face of darkness: How humor became humanity's oldest coping mechanism
content: Amidst hardship, dark humor emerges as a vital survival tool, offering a defiant spark against despair. This article explores how gallows humor, distinct from denial, helps individuals and communities cope with overwhelming realities. It highlights Gen Z's use of memes as a modern form of crisis comedy, underscoring laughter's profound psychological and biological benefits.

title: Crude beyond $120: Oil prices touch four-year peak, here's what analysts are saying
content: Global oil prices surged to a four-year high amid escalating US-Iran tensions and fears of a Strait of Hormuz closure, impacting gasoline prices and broader economies. Analysts warn of a potential global recession due to supply disruptions, with shortages already affecting various goods. Experts predict further price hikes until the strait reopens.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: Why Sooryavanshi was called 'unprofessional'? Jitesh Sharma reveals truth
content: Wicketkeeper Jitesh Sharma clarified his "unprofessional" comment about young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, explaining it stemmed from their close friendship. Sharma stressed that the 15-year-old is a typical teenager who enjoys simple pleasures. He urged against placing undue pressure on the youngster, advocating for him to enjoy his childhood while focusing on cricket.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 45: Ranveer Singh’s film eyes Rs 1790 cr globally
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1,138.54 crore in India net collections after 45 days. The film's 7th Saturday saw a significant surge, earning Rs 2.40 crore, pushing its gross worldwide total to Rs 1,788.32 crore. This success comes despite new releases, proving its enduring appeal.

title: After over 20K layoffs, how Oracle's 'promise' to Sam Altman is hurting banks across US
content: Oracle faces a significant bank-debt challenge due to its massive data centre expansion for OpenAI, reportedly requiring $300 billion. Lenders struggle with exposure limits, impacting their ability to finance these projects.  This comes as Oracle also plans substantial job cuts, highlighting financial pressures despite efforts to secure funding.

title: 'Not surprising': BJP MP Sandeep Pathak on 2 FIRs filed against him
content: Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak stated he has not received information about FIRs filed against him, suspecting political misuse. Punjab Police registered two non-bailable FIRs against Pathak after he switched from AAP to BJP. BJP and SAD leaders condemned the actions as political vendetta.

title: Candace Owens edits Erika Kirk AI gun thumbnail after backlash
content: Candace Owens is facing renewed backlash after quietly editing a controversial thumbnail linked to her latest episode. Critics claim the changes were subtle and failed to address the core issue, while supporters continue to defend her intent. The episode was also renamed and added to a series, raising further questions. The controversy has grown alongside reports of legal action against conspiracy-driven content tied to Erika Kirk and Charlie Kirk.

title: Watch: How Scientology churches across US became the latest target for Gen Z viral stunts
content: Around 50 teenagers forced their way into a Church of Scientology building in New York City, damaging property and injuring a security guard. The incident, linked to a social media trend called 'Scientology speed running,' involved coordinated trespass and aggression. Police are investigating similar disruptions reported in Los Angeles.

title: Minecraft Bedrock 26.30.25/26 beta: How to download latest version
content: Minecraft Bedrock 26.30.25/26 beta and preview is now available, featuring content from the upcoming Chaos Cubed update, including Sulfur Cube Archetypes and Geysers. This beta allows players to test new features before their official release. The article details how to download and install the beta on PC, Xbox, PlayStation, Android, and iOS devices.

title: Kerala verdict: Pinarayi Vijayan's welfare rule - will it fare well or get a farewell?
content: Kerala's upcoming election sees the LDF government banking on its development model and welfare gains for a third consecutive term, a feat rarely achieved due to anti-incumbency. Despite high SDG rankings, the state faces challenges in gender equality and economic growth, while migration and wage gaps emerge as key voter concerns.

title: Leather, footwear sector seeks duty relief as Middle East crisis drives up input costs
content: India's leather and footwear industry faces a 40-60% surge in input costs due to the Middle East crisis, impacting petroleum-based materials and imports. Industry representatives are urging the government for import duty exemptions on raw materials and machinery to mitigate these rising expenses and strengthen domestic manufacturing.

title: 'MPs who switched to BJP targeted': Chadha claims political vendetta, to meet Prez on May 5
content: Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha is set to meet President Droupadi Murmu on May 5 to voice concerns regarding the alleged misuse of state machinery in Punjab. He plans to highlight political vendetta against former AAP MPs who recently joined the BJP. Chadha, along with three other MPs, will present these issues to the President.

title: ‘Iron grills, locked terrace’: How Delhi residential building became its own death trap
content: A devastating pre-dawn fire in Delhi's Vivek Vihar claimed nine lives after trapping residents in a four-storey building.  Protective grills and a locked terrace blocked escape routes, hindering rescue efforts. The blaze, suspected to be from a short circuit, rapidly spread, leaving many unable to flee the inferno. Fifteen individuals were saved, but nine perished.

title: IPL: Mittal Family, Adar Poonawala acquire RR for approx $1.65 billion
content: A consortium led by Lakshmi Mittal, Aditya Mittal, and Adar Poonawala has acquired the IPL franchise Rajasthan Royals and its sister entities for approximately $1.65 billion. The deal, expected to conclude in Q3 2026, will see the Mittal family hold a majority stake. This acquisition follows a previous attempt by a Kal Somani-led consortium that faced funding and regulatory hurdles.

title: IIT-Ropar PhD scholar alleges assault, sexual harassment by supervisor
content: A PhD scholar at IIT-Ropar has accused her supervisor of physical assault and sexual harassment, alleging he punched her after she refused inappropriate demands and threatened her academic future. She claims surveillance was used against her. IIT-Ropar stated action was taken, including forced leave for the faculty member and police referral, while the student awaits an ICC report.

title: 'Cost between Rs 1,000 and Rs 5,000 per day': In Gujarat, bouncers hired to watch screen addicts
content: Desperate parents in Gujarat are hiring bouncers to manage teenagers battling severe phone, gaming, and drug addictions.  These security personnel, costing up to Rs 65,000 monthly, are deployed 24/7 to prevent violence, substance abuse, and risky behavior, highlighting a growing trend of extreme de-addiction measures.

title: Passenger held after opening emergency exit on moving flight at Chennai airport
content: A tense situation unfolded at Chennai International Airport when a passenger reportedly opened an emergency exit on an Air Arabia flight from Sharjah. The aircraft, carrying 231 people, was taxiing when the incident occurred. Security forces swiftly responded, detaining the 34-year-old passenger for questioning and assessment of his mental state.

title: 'What is there to be ashamed of?' Ashwin brings back Buttler 'Mankading' row
content: Seven years after the controversial 'Mankading' incident with Jos Buttler, Ravichandran Ashwin defends his actions, stating he did nothing wrong and played to win. He also reflected on his IPL career, expressing regret over not winning a title with Rajasthan Royals despite a fulfilling stint.

title: Markets this week: State poll verdicts, oil prices and Q4 earnings to steer mood
content: Markets brace for a crucial week as investors await state election results, with potential impacts on sentiment. Developments in the West Asia conflict and their influence on crude oil prices remain a significant concern for India's import-dependent economy. Key economic data and Q4 earnings will also shape market movements.

title: UP horror: Masked men enter baraat, shoot groom dead in Jaunpur
content: A 22-year-old groom was shot dead by three men on a motorcycle during his wedding procession in Jaunpur. The prime accused, Pradeep Bind, allegedly had a rivalry with the victim, Azad Bind, and had threatened him over the marriage. Police have registered a murder case and launched a search for the accused.

title: Mercedes in F1: Rise, fall, and rebirth of an empire
content: Mercedes dominated in an era of raw pace, then withdrew after a tragic accident. When they returned, they learned and built a seven-year period of dominance, after that a psychological setback. Now, they are experiencing yet another rebirth, with a completely different structure within the team.

title: BJP-dominant India or ... ? Decoding layers of saffron supremacy across country
content: The BJP's political dominance across India is undeniable, governing most states and the Centre. However, this influence varies significantly, with strongholds like Uttar Pradesh and Gujarat contrasting with regions where alliances are crucial. The party continues to expand its footprint, but challenges remain in the South and West Bengal, making upcoming elections a key test of its growth trajectory.

title: Ukraine hits two shadow fleet tankers carrying oil near Russia, launches 300+ drones
content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that the forces have struck two Russian oil tankers near Novorossiysk port. This action highlights Ukraine's ongoing strategy to disrupt Russia's energy sector. Drone warfare has intensified, with Ukraine increasingly targeting Russian territory. Russia has also conducted extensive drone attacks across Ukraine. Diplomatic efforts for a ceasefire are ongoing.

title: Pakistan faces economic strain; oil surge drives inflation toward 11%
content: Pakistan's economy faces sustained pressure with double-digit inflation likely to persist due to surging global oil prices amid the Middle East crisis. Rising energy costs and regional instability are projected to slow GDP growth and widen the current account deficit, impacting the stock market and currency.

title: 'No one is standing with Hardik Pandya': Ex-India star minces no words
content: Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 playoff hopes are all but over after an eight-wicket loss to Chennai Super Kings. Languishing in ninth place with two wins from nine matches, the star-studded team is struggling significantly. Former cricketers believe captain Hardik Pandya lacks support, and inconsistency across the squad is hindering results.

title: 'I will burn you, just watch': Bengaluru man pours petrol on son during fight
content: A father poured petrol on his 19-year-old son after an argument over a bike. Flames from nearby cooking caught the petrol-soaked youth, igniting him. The father also caught fire. Both sustained burn injuries and are undergoing treatment. Police have registered a case of attempt to murder.

title: 3-year-old rape-murder case in Pune: Massive protest on Mumbai-Bengaluru highway
content: Protesters blocked the Mumbai–Bengaluru highway in Pune for nearly four hours following the rape and murder of a three-year-old girl. Demonstrators placed the child's body on the road, demanding strict punishment for the 65-year-old accused, who has a prior criminal record. Authorities have assured a fast-track trial and the harshest punishment for the perpetrator.

title: 3am AC blast? Short circuit? How fire raced through locked, grilled Delhi Shahdara flats; 9 dead
content: A devastating fire, possibly triggered by an AC blast or short circuit, swept through a four-storey building in Vivek Vihar, east Delhi, killing nine people. The blaze, which started around 3:47 am, rapidly spread, trapping residents behind locked doors and grilled windows. Firefighters rescued about 15 people, but nine bodies were recovered from various floors.

title: Iranian super tanker slips past US blockade with $220 million oil cargo
content: Despite US claims of total control over the Strait of Hormuz and a successful naval blockade, an Iranian oil tanker carrying over 1.9 million barrels of crude has reportedly evaded US forces and reached Asia. This incident, alongside claims of numerous Iranian vessels breaching the blockade, highlights ongoing challenges in enforcing the US-imposed restrictions.

title: Chants of power: How slogans set the tone in Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam elections
content: Political parties in India's recent assembly elections heavily relied on catchy slogans to energize campaigns and shape narratives. From West Bengal's "Bhoy out, Bharosa in" to Tamil Nadu's "Stalin Thodaratum, Tamil Nadu Vellattum," these short phrases became central to electoral battles, reflecting regional identities and key campaign themes.

title: Market recap: Top 10 firms add Rs 2.20 lakh crore in value; Reliance biggest gainer
content: Dalal Street concluded the holiday-shortened week with gains, as the combined market valuation of India's top 10 firms surged by Rs 2.20 lakh crore, led by Reliance Industries. Despite a volatile trading environment and mixed global cues, investor sentiment remained resilient, with the BSE Sensex rising 0.32%.

title: "Death Was In Front Of Us": Jabalpur Boat Tragedy Survivors Recount Horror
content: A boat cruise accident at the Bargi Dam turned moments of joy into tragedy, leaving several families devastated. What began as a leisure outing quickly became a life-and-death situation as passengers struggled to survive.

title: Joram Aniya Becomes First Full-Time NITI Aayog Member From Arunachal
content: Dr Joram Anya is the first woman from the Nyishi community to earn a PhD and the first one in Arunachal Pradesh to complete the degree in Hindi.

title: 'Self-Funded Visit': NDTV Tracks Man In Mamata Banerjee's 'Outsider' Video
content: Deep Prajapati said his visit to West Bengal was self-funded and aimed at documenting what he described as the "ground reality" of the state.

title: Flier Vomits Twice, Jumps Off Moving Plane's Emergency Exit At Chennai Airport
content: An official said the passenger on the Sharjah-Chennai Air Arabia flight complained of a vomiting sensation and is believed to have vomited twice on board.

title: On Camera, 17-Year-Old Dies After Electrocution During Rain In Bengaluru
content: There was panic at the scene as bystanders rushed to help. However, they struggled to intervene due to the live current.

title: Video: Diljit Dosanjh Asks Pro-Khalistanis Waving Flag At Canada Concert To Leave
content: Singer Diljit Dosanjh's concerts have in the past seen pro-Khalistani flag-waving people try to disrupt the events

title: How Indian Start-up's Drishti Satellite Could Be New Headache For Pak, China
content: Suyash Singh, CEO and Founder of GalaxEye, called the mission a technological first and explained why India needed to think differently while designing satellites.

title: How Delhi Building Plan Became Death Sentence For Families Stuck In Fire
content: Though some managed to brave their way through the smoke-filled corridor and rushed to find their way to the terrace, the door was locked.

title: US Carriers Offer 'Rescue Fares' To Fliers As Spirit Airlines Winds Down
content: Spirit Airlines has been in and out of bankruptcy since 2024, and the White House was recently considering a bailout.

title: Over 50 Odisha Villagers Fall Ill After Having 'Dahi Bara' From Roadside Stall
content: Villagers ate dahi bara from a roadside vendor between 8:30 am and 9:30 am on Friday. Soon after, several of them complained of vomiting and diarrhoea.

title: Kerala's Political Future To Be Decided Tomorrow, Close Contest Likely: 10 Points
content: Kerala's political fate will be decided tomorrow with the counting of votes in the assembly election. Exit polls predicted a UDF win, ending Pinarayi Vijayan's ruling LDF's long journey since 2016. Health warning: exit polls often get it wrong.

title: "Wife Was Shouting, He Was Crying": Family Of Delhi Judge Who Died By Suicide
content: The judicial officer's family has claimed he had been under distress and had complained of harassment in the days leading up to his death.

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: India gets 'Drishti': An unblinking eye in the sky on Pak, China borders; PM Modi hails milestone
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More
